person.name: Sir John Wiltshire
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir John Wiltshire was a notable figure in the 14th and 15th centuries. Although his birth year is not precisely known, we can estimate it based on key timeline events. His first known appearance in historical records was in 1381, when he was charged with rescuing a prisoner from the custody of the Keeper of the Fleet. He was granted messuages in Calais with his wife in 1395, indicating that he was a property owner. During this period, he was granted knighthood. Therefore, Sir John Wiltshire was of age, well-established in high society, and involved in political matters.  It is a fair estimate then to suggest he was born about 1350 or before 1363, considering his first recorded event in 1381.  In addition to his military and diplomatic endeavors, Sir John Wiltshire was also a member of the aristocracy and had connections to several notable families. 

Do not merge this John Wiltshire with the Wiltshires of Stone Castle, whose profiles contain gross relationship errors. I have tried to unpack the correct relations based on genealogical standards, a reasonably exhaustive search, and a reconciliation of all genealogical resources, confirming the information below. There are simply impossibilities from internet trees and manipulation of dates to make it fit, creating a timeline that is earlier than actual timeline events creating false narratives not supported by site content submission policies, to include serial deletion without equal contribution.

 Sir John Wiltshire of Arundel and Calais married twice, first to Blanche de Mowbray, Lady Mowbray as her 5th husband. Blanche married Edward de Montagu in May 1343, Sir John de Worthe, Robert Bertram bef. Nov 1363, 2nd, Sir John de Worth, Thomas de Poynings bef. 5 Jun 1372, Sir John Wiltshire bef. 21 Mar 1377/78. 

 The first appearance of Sir John Wiltshire in records was associated with him being charged in 1381 with rescuing a prisoner from the custody of the Keeper of the Fleet. He obtained letters of protection in March 1386 and again in April 1398 and October 1403, suggesting that he may have been involved in other legal matters. Given the earliest known date of 1381, we can estimate, at minimum, he was of age (18) and thus born before 1363 and most likely about 1350. 

Sir John Wiltshire married Blanche de Mowbray, Lady Poynings, before November 5, 1394. This marriage would indicate that Sir John Wiltshire was a well-established man. Blanche Mowbray was a cousin of Thomas Fitz-Alan, Earl of Arundel. Certainly, this is cause for a further closeness of relations between Sir John Wiltshire and the Earl of Arundel.   Sir John Wiltshire was granted messuages in Calais with his wife in 1395, indicating he was a property owner. John Wiltshire also was knighted in 1395.  By 1396, The ransom of the Count of Denis occurred during the Battle of Nicopolis, which took place on September 25, 1396, in present-day Bulgaria. The battle was a crusade against the Ottoman Empire, led by Sigismund, Holy Roman Emperor. The Count of Denis and many other nobles were captured during the battle and held for ransom. 

Sir John Wiltshire had close ties to Thomas FitzAlan, Earl of Arundel and Surrey, and the Arundel family. He was present at the solemn promise of King Richard II to Richard, Earl of Arundel, shortly before the Earl's execution in 1397. His presence and stature were further evidence that Sir John Wiltshire’s involvement with the Arundel family was such that it was ordered that no harm would be done to him shortly before the Earl's execution in 1397.

Sir John's involvement in the ransom settlement of the Count of Denis was not his only connection to the Crusades. In 1398, he was one of several English knights who accompanied the Teutonic Knights on a campaign against the Ottoman Turks in Prussia. The Teutonic Knights were a medieval German military order that played a significant role in the Baltic Crusades. In 1402 he and Nicholas Hauberk, Knt. owed £266 to William Brestour, Citizen and saddler of London. In October 1403, he, John Sutheron, and John Waleys, Esq. obtained letters of general attorney.

Sir John Wiltshire was sent as an ambassador by Thomas, the Earl of Arundel, to promote the marriage of Queen Philippa of Portugal, the daughter of John of Gaunt, and his first wife, Blanche of Lancaster. Spring of 1405, Queen Philippa wrote a letter to her brother, King Henry IV of England, in which she mentioned Sir John Wiltshire: (“…Most high and puissant prince, my best-beloved brother, please it you to know that by Mr. John Wiltshire, knight, and ambassador of our cousin the Earl of Arundel”). Sir John's involvement in this diplomatic mission suggests that he had some expertise in international relations and was trusted by the Earl of Arundel to represent his interests.    In 1406 he was made a trustee of Earl Thomas’ widespread estates. Sir John Wiltshire’s first wife, Blanche, died on 21 July 1409. She left a will dated 16 July 1409, requesting burial at St. Pancras Priory, Lewes, Sussex. He married (2nd) Alice Heylesdon, widow of John Gurney, Esq. (died 1408), of Harpley, Drayton, and Heylesdon, Norfolk, and daughter and heiress of John de Heylesdon, of Heylesdon and Drayton, Norfolk, Citizen of London, by his wife, Joan.

He was also appointed a commissioner to settle a dispute respecting the ransom of the Count of Denis in 1412. The ransom of the Count of Denis occurred during the Battle of Nicopolis, which took place back on September 25, 1396.

In 1415 he was nominated an executor of the will of Thomas, Earl of Arundel, which further affirms the long-standing closeness of their relationship and trust. In 1422 he and other Arundel feoffees demised the manor of Bignor, Sussex, to Beatrice, Countess of Arundel, for life. In 1423–1424 he and other Arundel feoffees demised the estates of South Stoke, Warningcamp, etc., to John Clamorde, master, and the chaplains of the college in the parish church of Arundel founded by Richard, late Earl of Arundel. 

Sir John Wiltshire held a lordship called Fairchild in the reign of Henry Vl. His will was dated at Haylesdon, November 17, 1424, and proved May 25, 1428. He gave his tenants here 40s and required that he be buried in the priory of Lewes in Sussex by his late wife, Blanche, and named John, his son, and Alice, his wife, as executors.  This further affirms this genealogist’s understanding that he consecutively married Blanche de Mowbray and Alice Heylesdon as his first and second wives.   John Wiltshire had his son John Wiltshire by his first wife, as she was alive in 1409, and his son is estimated to be born before this date.  His son, Sir John Wiltshire of Arundel and Lyminster, married Isabell Clothall.    Sir John Wiltshire died before 13 Feb. 1428 and was buried in Lewes Priory, Sussex. His widow, Alice, married 3rd, as his 2nd wife, Richard Selling (or Sellyng), Esq., of Heylesdon, Norfolk.

Sir John Wiltshire’s name appears in St. Le Kniveton's Collection for a History of the Earls of Arundel, suggesting that he played some role in the family's history. He was also mentioned in a manuscript relating to the history of the Earls of Arundel. The pedigree of Beatrice, Countess of Arundel, who died in 1439, and noting that Sir John Wiltshire was deceased before this date.   Sir John Wiltshire of Calais was the same person as Sir John Wiltshire of Arundel, indicating that he held property in both places.

Sir John's legacy lived on through his descendants. His son, Sir John Wiltshire of Arundel and Lyminster, was a Member of Parliament for Sussex in 1449 and served as High Sheriff of Sussex in 1460. Sir John's grandson, also named John Wiltshire, was a prominent figure in Sussex politics and served as High Sheriff of Sussex in 1485 and 1499.

sourceList.title: The History of Parliament, GURNEY, John (d.1408), of Harpley and West Barsham, Norf.
Arundel Borough Members of Parliament, ublished in The History of Parliament: the House of Commons 1386-1421, ed. J.S. Roskell, L. Clark, C. Rawcliffe.
The Politics of Mercy: The Use of the Royal Pardon in Fourteenth-Century England, by Helen Lacy, Submitted for the degree of PhD in Medieval Studies

zh.Hellesdon, Norfolk, England, United King: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可海勒達
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Wiltshire於1372年之前出生在英國時，他的父親John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II42歲，母親Joan Kelleshull16歲。他於1394年11月5日之前與Blanche Mowbray結婚。他於1413年在英國素可阿盧德爾被列為member of parliament for arundel。他去世於1428年2月13日之前在英國英格蘭諾福爾可海勒達，埋葬在英國素可列維斯。

zh-hans.Hellesdon, Norfolk, England, United King: 英国英格兰諾福爾可海勒達
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Wiltshire于1372年之前出生在英国时，他的父亲John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II42岁，母亲Joan Kelleshull16岁。他于1394年11月5日之前与Blanche Mowbray结婚。他于1413年在英国素可阿盧德爾被列为member of parliament for arundel。他去世于1428年2月13日之前在英国英格兰諾福爾可海勒達，埋葬在英国素可列維斯。

nameMeanings.name: Wiltshire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Wiltshire in southwest central England, which gets its name from Wilton (once the county's principal town) + Old English scīr ‘shire, administrative division’.

variants.name: Wilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places so named, including Wilton (Wiltshire, North Yorkshire), Wilton in Haile (Cumberland), and Wilton in Bridstow (Herefordshire). The North Yorkshire placename derives from Old English wilde ‘wild, uncultivated’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Wiltshire placename may derive from Old English wull ‘wool’ + tūn. The Cumberland and Herefordshire placenames derive from Old English wilig ‘willow’ + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Wiltshire was born before 1372, in England, his father, John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II, was 42 and his mother, Joan Kelleshull, was 16. He married Blanche Mowbray before 5 November 1394. In 1413, his occupation is listed as member of parliament for arundel in Arundel, Sussex, England. He died before 13 February 1428, in Hellesdon, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Lewes, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Wiltshire est né avant 1372, à Angleterre, son père, John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Joan Kelleshull, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Blanche Mowbray avant 5 novembre 1394. En 1413, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament for arundel à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 13 février 1428, à Hellesdon, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Wiltshire vor 1372 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II 42 und seine Mutter Joan Kelleshull 16. Er hat vor 5. November 1394 Blanche Mowbray geheiratet. 1413 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament for arundel in Arundel, Sussex, England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 13. Februar 1428 in Hellesdon, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lewes, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Wiltshire è nato il prima del 1372 nel England, il suo padre, John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Joan Kelleshull, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Blanche Mowbray prima del 5 novembre 1394. Nel 1413, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament for arundel nel Arundel, Sussex, England. Morì prima del 13 febbraio 1428, nel Hellesdon, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Lewes, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Wiltshireが1372年以前にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard IIは42歳，母Joan Kelleshullは16歳であった。1394年11月5日以前にBlanche Mowbrayと結婚した。1413年，イギリススセックスアルンデルでの職業はmember of parliament for arundelと記録されている。彼は1428年2月13日以前にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヘレスドンで死去し，イギリススセックスルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Wiltshire(이)가 1372년 이전 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Kelleshull(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1394년 11월 5일 이전 Blanche Mowbray(와)과 결혼했다. 1413년, 그의 직업은 영국 수세스 아런들에서 member of parliament for arundel이었다. 그는 1428년 2월 13일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 헬레즈던에서 사망했으며 영국 수세스 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.from 22 June 1393 to 21 June 1394: de 22 de junho de 1393 a 21 de junho de 1394
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Wiltshire nasceu antes 1372, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Joan Kelleshull, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Blanche Mowbray antes 5 de novembro de 1394. Em 1413, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament for arundel em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 13 de fevereiro de 1428, em Hellesdon, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Wiltshire родился перед 1372 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Wiltshire , Citizen, grocer, pepperer-spice merchant of London, of Heydon, Tenant to Richard II было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Kelleshull, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Blanche Mowbray перед 5 ноябрь 1394 г. В 1413 году он работал по профессии member of parliament for arundel в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия. Он умер перед 13 февраль 1428 г. в местности хеллесдон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия.

Se casó con Blanche Mowbray antes de 5 de noviembre de 1394. En 1413, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament for arundel en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra. Murió antes de 13 de febrero de 1428, en Hellesdon, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.
